dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 9730 IH: Foreign Service Test-Free Reentry Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Beyer (for himself, Mr. Meeks, Mr. Walkinshaw, Ms. Titus, Mr. Olszewski, and Ms. Norton) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs

form.official-title: To expedite the rehiring of certain former career members of the Foreign Service, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Foreign Service Test-Free Reentry Act of 2026.

section.header: Expedited rehiring of former career members
section.text: Section 308 of the Foreign Service Act of 1980 (22 U.S.C. 3948) is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

paragraph.text: With respect to any former career member of the Service involuntarily separated or retired as part of a reduction in force or other expedited retirement process during the period beginning January 20, 2025, and ending January 31, 2030, who was serving in good standing as of the date of such separation or retirement, the Secretary may reappoint such former career member without requiring any written or oral examination or other threshold qualification described in section 301.

subparagraph.text: the term serving in good standing, with respect to an individual who was separated or retired, means that the individual did not receive any low rankings within the 5 years prior to their separation or retirement date to the extent that such review with respect to such individual is clearly documented; and
the authority provided under paragraph (1) may not be exercised with respect to any former career service member that was not involuntarily separated or retired—

clause.text: during the period described in that paragraph; and
as part of a reduction in force or other expedited retirement process.
